talk_metadata.talk_title: Epigenomic heterogeneity in metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have no evidence that increased accessibility of LINE1 elements also promotes ecDNA generation"
Speaker said: "we have no evidence that increased transcription of LINEs occurs"
Speaker said: "we cannot give you the answer yet"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: LINE‑1+ chromatin variant subgroup drives AR overexpression via repeat‑derived regulatory elements
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: LINE‑1+ are intrinsically resistant to androgen receptor selective inhibitors

key_data_gaps: No functional validation of repeat‑derived enhancers

crispro_opportunity.description: LINE‑1+ chromatin variants create repeat‑derived enhancers that boost AR expression, suggesting a biomarker‑guided strategy to combine AR degraders with agents targeting repeat‑derived regulatory activity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: LINE‑1+ are intrinsically resistant to androgen receptor selective inhibitors ... high density of de novo activated regulatory elements working at that locus to enhance the expression of the androgen receptor.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model repeat‑derived enhancer‑target interactions and predict compounds that disrupt AR‑driven transcriptional programs.

crispro_opportunity.description: Patients can be stratified by LINE‑1+ chromatin variant signature to predict response to AR‑targeted therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Classification based on chromatin variants over repetitive sequences identifies a LINE‑1+ subgroup with poor response to AR inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop a predictive classifier integrating ATAC‑seq repeat accessibility with clinical outcomes.

cited_competitors.name: Androgen receptor selective inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Standard of care drugs used in the cohort

unresolved_questions: What is the causal mechanism linking LINE‑1 accessibility to AR overexpression?
Do repeat‑derived enhancers directly drive ecDNA formation?

talk_metadata.talk_title: SMARCA4 loss drives dedifferentiated endometrial cancer via epigenetic plasticity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we are careful about this because we don't think it's inducing dedifferentiation per se"
Speaker said: "we think that SMARCA4 enables cell state disorder"
Speaker said: "we have no embryonic signature"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: SMARCA4 loss creates uncommitted cell states that confer chemotherapy resistance
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: tumors became resistant to carboplatin after SMARCA4 loss

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that SMARCA4 loss reduced tumorigenicity in vitro yet concluded that SMARCA4 loss drives aggressive dedifferentiated endometrial cancer in vivo

key_data_gaps: Lack of in vivo validation in immunocompetent models

crispro_opportunity.description: Targeting the uncommitted cell state (e.g., epigenetic modulators) may sensitize SMARCA4‑deficient tumors to chemotherapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: SMARCA4 loss enables cell state disorder ... tumors became resistant to carboplatin.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synthetic lethal interactions with SWI/SNF deficiency and prioritize epigenetic drugs.

unresolved_questions: What upstream signals trigger the epigenetic drift observed after SMARCA4 loss?
Can immune‑competent models recapitulate the phenotype?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Adenosquamous transition (AST) in KRAS‑mutant lung cancer and therapeutic strategies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see a high enrichment of squamous signature correlates with shorter treatment duration"
Speaker said: "NF‑κB activation is transient but essential for adenocarcinoma survival"
Speaker said: "IL‑1α secreted by P63+ cells reprograms CAFs"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: AST to squamous phenotype driven by ROS‑NF‑κB‑IL‑1 axis bypasses KRAS dependence
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: once they transition to squamous, they are become resistant to the RAS oncogene inhibitor

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: KRAS G12C inhibition in LKB1‑mutant lung adenocarcinoma
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all LKB1‑mutant adenocarcinomas without stratifying for squamous signature or ROS status
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Squamous lineage signature / ROS/NF‑κB activation markers
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial biopsies to capture AST emergence

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Efficacy limited by lineage plasticity (AST) that bypasses KRAS signaling
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing KRAS G12C monotherapy in LKB1‑mutant tumors without addressing plasticity

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that NF‑κB inhibition accelerates squamous transition yet concluded that transient NF‑κB activation is required to maintain adenocarcinoma lineage

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective validation of KLK6A as predictive biomarker

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a KLK6A‑based assay to identify patients at risk of AST before KRAS G12C therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: high KLK6A level really correlate with poor patient survival and squamous signature enrichment
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate transcriptomic and epigenomic data to generate a predictive score for AST risk.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine KRAS G12C inhibitors with NF‑κB modulators or IL‑1 receptor antagonists to prevent AST.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: NF‑κB inhibition accelerates squamous transition; IL‑1 receptor antagonist reduces NETs and squamous tumor burden.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model pathway interactions and prioritize combination regimens that suppress the ROS‑NF‑κB‑IL‑1 axis.

crispro_opportunity.description: Design trials that stratify by LKB1 status and include serial biopsies to monitor squamous signature emergence.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Correlation of squamous signature with poor response only observed in LKB1‑mutant subgroup.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate adaptive trial designs incorporating dynamic biomarker monitoring.

cited_competitors.name: Androgen receptor selective inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as comparison for resistance mechanisms

unresolved_questions: What is the precise trigger linking ROS accumulation to NF‑κB activation in LKB1‑deficient tumors?
Can IL‑1β blockade alone prevent NET‑mediated squamous tumor growth?
